current_state.summary: PLACEHOLDER — the handler body is a single logger.debug() call and a comment: 'Placeholder: future implementation will check separation-of-duties violations'. No engine is called. No action is taken.
current_state.intended_purpose: Check Separation of Duties (SoD) violations when any identity is created, modified, or granted new access.
current_state.comment_in_code: # Placeholder: future implementation will check separation-of-duties violations

what_works: Event registration is in place — IDENTITY_UPDATED events are received by the subscriber.
org_id is extracted from event payload.
what_doesnt: THE ENTIRE HANDLER IS A PLACEHOLDER — no engine is called, no action is taken when an identity is updated.
AccessGovernanceEngine.check_sod_violations() is not called — SoD violations are never automatically detected on identity change.
IdentityRiskEngine is not notified — identity risk profiles are never recomputed after role or permission changes.
InsiderThreatEngine is not notified — privilege escalation events that should trigger insider threat signals are silently dropped.
ITDREngine (Identity Threat Detection & Response) is not triggered — the primary use case for identity-aware threat response is bypassed.
UserAccessReviewEngine is not notified — pending access reviews are not flagged when the reviewed identity changes.
IDENTITY_UPDATED is missing from the legacy EventBus type_map.
Identity changes are the highest-risk events in IAM security (lateral movement, privilege escalation) — having a no-op subscriber here is a significant security intelligence gap.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: Given IDENTITY_UPDATED with user_id and new_roles present, AccessGovernanceEngine SoD check is triggered for the updated identity.

AC2: Given IDENTITY_UPDATED where a SoD violation is detected, an ANOMALY_DETECTED event is emitted with entity_type='sod_violation', user_id, and severity='high' so the anomaly fan-out triggers InsiderThreatEngine.
AC3: Given IDENTITY_UPDATED, IdentityRiskEngine recomputes the risk profile for the affected identity.
AC4: Given IDENTITY_UPDATED with change_type='privilege_escalation', ITDREngine is notified with the escalation details for threat response evaluation.
AC5: Given IDENTITY_UPDATED for an identity currently under an active UserAccessReview, the access review item must be flagged as 'identity_changed_during_review' for reviewer attention.
AC6: IDENTITY_UPDATED must be added to the legacy EventBus type_map.
AC7: All downstream engine calls must be in separate _safe_call() wrappers.
AC8: The placeholder comment and logger.debug() must be replaced with real implementation.
AC9: A pytest test verifies: IDENTITY_UPDATED → SoD check called, IdentityRiskEngine called, and SoD violation triggers ANOMALY_DETECTED emission.
AC10: For bulk identity changes (e.g. directory sync importing 1,000 users), downstream calls must be batched or rate-limited to prevent thundering-herd on SoD and risk engines.
